actionDef.name: AeriesParentsToRI_leb
actionDef.description: Account Provisioning receives creation, updates, and disables from source data and processes into RapidIdentity

argDef.description: TRUE: Forces the action set to skip the DB timestamp check
argDef.name: overrideTimestampCheck

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Needed to have a centralized store for subject lines

arg.name: comment
arg.value: These two globals should be stored as follow

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Connect To Portal and RapidIdentity. Send out notifications on failed connections

arg.name: value
arg.value:
"errorMessage : "+ riErrorMessage + "
" + "errorCode : "+ riErrorCode + "
" + "actionName : "+ actionSetName + "
" + "jobName : "+ processJobName + "
" + "jobStartTime : "+ processStartTime + "
" + "jobLogFile : "+ processLogFile

arg.name: sql
arg.value:
"select [mins_since_last_exec] = datediff(minute, max(l.EndDate), current_timestamp)

from dbo.ToAeries_SyncLog as l

where l.SyncIsOk = 1"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Writeback database timestamp is older than 60 minutes. Exiting."

arg.name: message
arg.value: "The action set AeriesParentsToRI has aborted due to an inavlid DB timestamp. Please review Connect logs for further details."

arg.name: value
arg.value:
"errorMessage : "+ riErrorMessage + "
" + "errorCode : "+ riErrorCode + "
" + "actionName : "+ actionSetName + "
" + "jobName : "+ processJobName + "
" + "jobStartTime : "+ processStartTime + "
" + "jobLogFile : "+ processLogFile

arg.name: comment
arg.value: any mapping files should be included here and parsed

arg.name: comment
arg.value: CHANGE FIELDS BELOW TO MATCH ENVIRONMENT!!!!!!

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Only process school codes less than 400 and not 0

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Only process parents with specifc attributes in the source

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Store StudentID + SchoolCode to ensure we can locate the student when setting challenge questions

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Store StudentID + SchoolCode to ensure we can locate the student when setting challenge questions

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Store StudentID + SchoolCode to ensure we can locate the student when setting challenge questions

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Filter to only the records we need

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Client Specific Changes and attributes Should Happen here

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Out of the box we will only clear white space

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Other use cases... Proper Case Fields, strings split etc...

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Retrieve All the record fields, and clear out any whitespace

arg.name: comment
arg.value: This removes the value therefore we send over a null value

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Search for the user by idautoPersonSTUID or idautoPersonHRID. If another uniqueID is used then specify it here

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Do not use a wildcard here. The index is set for an equality index

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Add in more crieteria Here for anyconditions that cannot be met

arg.name: comment
arg.value: the status needs to be changed based on the system. idautoDisabled is ONLY updated in the appropriate sections. This allows for the section to determine the appropriate account status

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Ensure duplicate parents are not created

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "SELECT * FROM ToAeries_Parent WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ?"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent record with matching student ID and sequence number already exists in the database. Skipping creation: " + record

arg.name: comment
arg.value: The only exception would be a default claim code which is dependent on how the end users will initially log in

arg.name: comment
arg.value: the default ou for all accounts is ou=accounts. There is no longer a distinguisher of ous

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Default Update attributes from the source

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Ensure single user mode doesnt wipe out all students from the existing record

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Check if another account already is using personal email address

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Setting new personal email due to collision: " + record['idautoPersonSAMAccountName'] + "@parents.springisd.local"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Setting new personal email due to collision: " + record['idautoPersonSAMAccountName'] + "@parents.springisd.local"

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Record Has Changed is just a reference. Copy them solely for comparison therefore you do not accidentally modify the record

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Check for a rename based on ONLY the last name. This only sets the flag and can be incorporated into the template

arg.name: comment
arg.value: This will only set the flag to rename an account A separate action set should be used to do the rename.

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Set the value of updates to the changedValues for logging

arg.name: comment
arg.value: After validating all other conditions add the record to fields needing to be updated

arg.name: comment
arg.value: The treshold should be set to make sure there is a minimum number of records returned. If we do not care about this then set the amount to 1

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Calculate the Threshold amount as a percentage

arg.name: comment
arg.value: anyplace where id is referenced, keyField and keyValue will need to be added to account for differences from the source

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Setting new personal email due to collision: " + addRecord['idautoPersonSAMAccountName'] + "@parents.springisd.local"

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Create The account without a password

arg.name: comment
arg.value: add the password below as a best practice using the built in function

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "SELECT * FROM ToAeries_Parent WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ?"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "UPDATE ToAeries_Parent SET isActive = 1, Email = ?, Username = ? WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ?"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent successfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent unsuccessfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent successfully written back to Database: INSERT"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent successfully written back to Database: INSERT"

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Prevent unwanted deletions of parent accounts that were not successfully written back.

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "SELECT * FROM ToAeries_Parent WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ?"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "UPDATE ToAeries_Parent SET isActive = 1, Email = ?, Username = ? WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ?"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent successfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent unsuccessfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent successfully written back to Database: INSERT"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent unsuccessfully written back to Database: INSERT"

arg.name: comment
arg.value: Prevent unwanted deletions of parent accounts that were not successfully written back.

arg.name: message
arg.value: "User Not Created - RapidIdentity : " + addRecord['idautoPersonStuID'] + " - Error : " + addErrorMessage

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 this gate includes idautoPersonSAMAccountName, and idautoPersonHomeEmail now

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "SELECT * FROM ToAeries_Parent WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ?"

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 first do Username/isActive only (no Email in this UPDATE) - Combining them sometimes left Email sale when username changed. This caused the "flip-backs"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "UPDATE ToAeries_Parent SET Username = ?, isActive = 1 WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ?"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent successfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent unsuccessfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 Add a second, collision-aware Email gate - Prefer the real RI email unless a different Username already owns it (step-parent edgecase)

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "SELECT Username FROM ToAeries_Parent WHERE LOWER(Email) = LOWER(?) LIMIT 1"

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 SAME username already owns the real email - okay to keep/use it here

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 someone else (different Username) owns this real email - generic email should be used to prevent collision

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 nobody owns it -> use the real email

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 no real email on the RI record - Use the generic

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "UPDATE ToAeries_Parent SET Email = ? WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ?"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent successfully written back to Database: UPDATE Email -> "+ targetEmail

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent unsuccessfully written back to Database: UPDATE Email"

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 Insert path applies the SAME collision rule so inserts match updates

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "SELECT Username FROM ToAeries_Parent WHERE LOWER(Email) = LOWER(?) LIMIT 1"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "INSERT INTO ToAeries_Parent (StudentID, Username, Email, isActive, Sequence) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?)"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent successfully written back to Database: INSERT"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent unsuccessfully written back to Database: INSERT"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "SELECT * FROM ToAeries_Parent WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ?"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "UPDATE ToAeries_Parent SET Username = ?, isActive = 1 WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ?"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent successfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent unsuccessfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "SELECT Username FROM ToAeries_Parent WHERE LOWER(Email) = LOWER(?) LIMIT 1"

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 add a log for when collision happens

arg.name: message
arg.value: "INSERT Email collision -> using alias for " + desiredUsername

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 nobody owns it -> use the real email

arg.name: comment
arg.value: leb 09232025 00154617 no real email on the RI record - Use the generic

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent successfully written back to Database: INSERT"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent unsuccessfully written back to Database: INSERT"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "SELECT * FROM ToAeries_Parent WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ? AND Username = ?"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "UPDATE ToAeries_Parent SET Email = ? WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ? AND Username = ?"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent email successfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent email unsuccessfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "SELECT * FROM ToAeries_Parent WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ? AND Username = ?"

arg.name: sql
arg.value: "UPDATE ToAeries_Parent SET Email = ? WHERE StudentID = ? AND Sequence = ? AND Username = ?"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent email successfully written back to Database: UPDATE"

arg.name: message
arg.value: "Parent email unsuccessfully written back to Database: UPDATE"
